senses.definition: the symbol @, that is used in email addresses and other forms of electronic communication
a file or document that is sent along with an email

examples.x: She sent an attachment with the report to her boss.
He opened the attachment to review the presentation slides.
They forgot to include the attachment in their message.
The attachment contained important information for the project.
She saved the attachment to her computer for later use.
They found the attachment to be corrupted and could not open it.

senses.definition: used on a business letter or email indicating that a copy is being sent to the person mentioned

examples.x: Please add John and Sarah to the CC so they can stay informed about the project updates.
The boss was included in the CC to ensure they were aware of the ongoing discussions.
In email etiquette, it's important to use CC appropriately to keep relevant parties informed.
The email chain grew longer as more people were added to the CC.

senses.definition: an unfinished version of something written that might be subject to change before it is finished
offensive and often threatening letters or emails usually sent under no name

examples.x: She received hate mail after speaking out on social media.
He found hate mail in his inbox, filled with negative comments.
They reported the hate mail to the authorities for safety.
The politician was targeted with hate mail during the campaign.
He decided to ignore the hate mail and focus on his supporters.

senses.definition: a folder in which received emails or text messages are stored

examples.x: I have over 100 unread messages in my email inbox.
She checks her inbox every morning to stay updated on work emails.
The notification popped up in his inbox as soon as the message was sent.
He organized his inbox by creating folders for different projects.
The spam filter moved the suspicious email out of the inbox.

senses.definition: a list of names and email addresses stored on a computer that one can send mail at the same time to all of them
a place where unsent emails are stored
unwanted or irrelevant online advertisements sent to many people

examples.x: The email filter automatically detects and removes spam from my inbox.
He received a lot of spam in his email account after signing up for a free trial.
The company's IT policy includes guidelines for reporting and handling spam.

senses.definition: an email feature that allows you to send a copy of a message to recipients without revealing their identities to others

examples.x: I sent the newsletter to the team using BCC to keep everyone's email addresses private.
I accidentally forgot to use BCC when emailing a large group, and now everyone can see each other's email addresses.
The manager used BCC to send the invitation to all employees without revealing their contacts.
For privacy, always use BCC when sending group emails.

senses.definition: a message, email, or communication that you have received, usually to provide an answer, acknowledge receipt, or continue a conversation
an item or message marked with a visual indicator to signify its importance, status, or need for follow-up

examples.x: She placed a flag on the email to remind herself to respond later.
In the project management tool, he added a flag to tasks that required priority.
The moderator added a flag to inappropriate comments for review.

senses.definition: letters, emails, etc. received by a media organization or an important individual at a specific time or about a specific subject
(computing) an offensive or violent e-mail sent to someone usually in quick response
the space at the top of an email that indicates its subject

examples.x: The subject line of her email was so clear that I opened it right away.
He added "Urgent" to the subject line to get my attention.
I always check the subject line first before deciding whether to read the email.
The subject line seemed too vague, so I almost ignored the email.

senses.definition: the metadata and routing information of an email, including sender and recipient details, subject, and date

examples.x: The email header showed that it was sent by my friend from her personal account.
I accidentally overlooked the email header and missed an important detail.
She accidentally sent the email to the wrong person because she didn't check the email header.
When I checked the email header, I saw it was sent at 3:00 AM.

senses.definition: a section at the end of an email or document that includes the sender's name, contact information, job title, and other relevant details, often used for personal or professional identification and to provide contact information

examples.x: Her email signature block includes her title, company, and office address.
Before sending the email, make sure the signature block is correctly formatted.
I updated my signature block with my new job title and email address.
My signature block always includes my name, position, and website for quick reference.

senses.definition: an email that failed to be delivered to the intended recipient due to various reasons, such as an invalid or non-existent email address, a full mailbox, or the recipient's mail server issues
a unique identifier consisting of a user's username and the domain name of the email service provider, allowing users to send and receive electronic messages over the Internet

examples.x: Please provide your email address so we can send you the confirmation details.
She forgot to update her email address in the company's contact directory.
The sign-up form required me to enter my email address to create an account.
He shared his new email address with friends after changing service providers.
Remember to double-check the recipient's email address before hitting send.

senses.definition: a regularly distributed electronic publication that is sent to subscribers via email, containing updates, news, articles, promotions, or other content related to a specific topic, organization, or interest

examples.x: I signed up for the email newsletter to get updates on the latest tech trends.
The company sends out a weekly email newsletter with special offers and product news.
He unsubscribed from the email newsletter because he was getting too many spam emails.
She always reads the email newsletter from the local bookstore to find out about new book releases.

senses.definition: a series of related email messages that are grouped together in a conversation, displaying the entire communication history between multiple participants on a particular topic or subject

examples.x: I need to check the email thread before responding to make sure I did not miss any important details.
The email thread is getting long, so I’ll summarize the key points for everyone.
Please reply to the email thread, so we keep all our communication in one place.
The email thread shows that the meeting has been rescheduled for next Wednesday.
